skill.name: opensource-guide-coach
skill.description: Guides users through open source project decisions using the official Open Source Guides framework.
skill.summary: Coaches maintainers and contributors on open source launch, growth, governance, sustainability, and community health.
skill.author: xixu-me

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found only Markdown backticks, official URLs, guide titles, and topic words. There are no scripts, shell calls, data collection, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False positive external command matches in Markdown
low_findings.description: Backticks are used for Markdown formatting of guide slugs, counts, filenames, and response examples. No shell, Ruby, or process execution API is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is plain Markdown content and inline code formatting. There is no interpreter, command runner, script file, or instruction to execute user-controlled input.

low_findings.title: False positive network matches from official source links
low_findings.description: The URLs point to official Open Source Guides and GitHub source pages for attribution and user-facing citations. They are static references and do not perform network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links only. No code path issues HTTP requests, reads secrets, or sends repository data to external endpoints.

low_findings.title: False positive weak cryptography and reconnaissance matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain prose about Open Source Guides, legal basics, security guide topics, and quality guidance. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, host discovery, or system reconnaissance behavior is implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review shows content-only guidance, not operational security tooling. The high static severity comes from keyword matches inside normal documentation text.

content.value_statement: Open source teams often struggle to choose the right next step for launch, growth, governance, or sustainability. This skill maps the situation to official Open Source Guides and turns them into a practical action plan.
actual_capabilities: Routes open source questions to the most relevant official guide topics.
Infers the likely user persona and project stage from the prompt.
Creates prioritized next steps for launch, growth, governance, funding, and maintainer balance.
Links recommendations to canonical Open Source Guides URLs.
Frames legal and security topics as general guidance, not professional advice.
Drafts requested contributor or governance artifacts when the user asks for them.
limitations: Does not replace legal counsel for licensing or policy decisions.
Does not provide deep code-level security implementation support.
Depends on the official Open Source Guides as its primary source.
May need project-specific details for precise governance or community recommendations.

use_cases.title: Prepare a First Open Source Release
use_cases.description: Get a launch checklist, contributor expectations, and source links before publishing a personal project.

use_cases.title: Reduce Maintainer Overload
use_cases.description: Identify lightweight process changes that lower issue pressure and protect maintainer time.

use_cases.title: Formalize Community Practices
use_cases.description: Plan governance, behavior expectations, and contributor onboarding for a growing shared project.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Launch Check
prompt_templates.scenario: A first-time maintainer wants to open source a project.
prompt_templates.prompt: I built a small library and want to open source it. What should I prepare before launch?

prompt_templates.title: Contributor Onboarding Review
prompt_templates.scenario: A project has users but few contributors.
prompt_templates.prompt: Our project has users, but new contributors rarely make a first pull request. Help me improve the contributor path.

prompt_templates.title: Maintainer Burnout Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: A maintainer needs practical boundaries and process changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: I maintain several open source projects and feel overloaded by issues and review requests. Recommend a realistic plan.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Governance Planning
prompt_templates.scenario: A growing project needs decision rules and community norms.
prompt_templates.prompt: Our community project is growing and decisions are becoming unclear. Help us choose governance and conduct practices without overbuilding process.

output_examples.input: Should I open source my side project now?
output_examples.output: A concise situation assessment, one to three relevant guide links, a launch readiness checklist, watch-outs, and optional deeper reading.

output_examples.input: We need more contributors but do not want to create too much process.
output_examples.output: A contributor funnel diagnosis with lightweight next steps for issues, documentation, welcome signals, and community expectations.

output_examples.input: Our maintainers disagree about who decides roadmap priorities.
output_examples.output: A governance-focused response that explains the likely project stage, recommends official guide topics, and proposes proportionate decision rules.

best_practices: Share the project stage, maintainer capacity, and main pain point in your prompt.
Ask for a checklist when you need decisions before drafting documents.
Request a specific artifact only after you know which guide topics apply.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to provide binding legal advice or license approval.
Do not use it as a substitute for incident response or code security review.
Do not request a full governance system for a small project without explaining the need.

faq.question: What sources does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses the official Open Source Guides and local reference maps included with the skill.

faq.question: Can it draft a CONTRIBUTING file?
faq.answer: Yes, when you explicitly ask for that artifact and provide enough project context.

faq.question: Does it give legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It can explain general open source legal concepts and recommend when to consult a lawyer.

faq.question: Can it help with maintainer burnout?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest boundaries, triage practices, and next steps based on maintainer balance guidance.

faq.question: Does it browse the web automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains reference links, but it does not include code that makes network requests.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
